channel.title: The Grant Desk
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk.

item.title: Nationality for All: SEAP Fellows 2026 (Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific) - AUD 200-400 per month depending on the fellow's location. This is a low-stipend research fellowship; the work itself is full-time over three months. - deadline 25 May 2026
item.description:
Nationality for All (NFA)

Location: Fully remote; applicants must be early-career human rights professionals based in the Asia Pacific region

Award: AUD 200-400 per month depending on the fellow's location. This is a low-stipend research fellowship; the work itself is full-time over three months.

Duration: 3 months full-time, 15 June - 11 September 2026 (remote)

Deadline: 25 May 2026

Inaugural 2026 SEAP Fellowship Programme from Nationality for All (NFA) for early-career human rights professionals based in the Asia Pacific region. Two full-time, three-month remote fellows will support the updating and verification of the Second Edition of the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP) - a regional knowledge resource on statelessness, nationality rights, legal identity and exclusion in the Asia-Pacific region. The three sub-regions (Southeast Asia, East Asia, Central Asia) are divided between the two fellows. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: factchecking and additional desk research on existing SEAP country factsheets; updating SEAP website and report content; integrating and verifying citations across SEAP outputs. TIMELINE: Month 1 onboarding and factchecking; Month 2 website and report updates; Month 3 citation integration. ELIGIBILITY: early-career human rights professionals based in the Asia Pacific region with strong desk research, writing and organisational skills; background in human rights, law, public policy, international relations or related disciplines; knowledge of citizenship rights, statelessness and legal identity systems is desirable but not mandatory. Persons with lived experience of statelessness will be prioritised. SUPPORT: AUD 200-400 per month stipend depending on fellow's location (low-stipend research fellowship); training in factchecking, citation standards and website systems; ongoing mentorship from the NFA research team. APPLY: send CV, cover letter and one original writing sample to info@nationalityforall.org with subject line 'Application - SEAP Fellows 2026' by 25 May 2026 (EOD Bangkok time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Taiwan AID: 2026 NGO Fellowship Program (Asia and Pacific Islands) - Fully funded: round-trip airfare, accommodation (dormitory-style rooms, 6-8 beds per room), meals, and local transportation in Taiwan are all covered. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium. - deadline 01 Jun 2026
item.description:
Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID)

Location: Taipei, Taiwan (fully on-site participation required, including a three-week field placement at a Taiwanese NGO)

Award: Fully funded: round-trip airfare, accommodation (dormitory-style rooms, 6-8 beds per room), meals, and local transportation in Taiwan are all covered. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Four weeks, 4 October - 1 November 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID) annual fellowship for NGO professionals from South Asia, Southeast Asia, Northeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. Approximately 12 fellows selected (tentative) for a four-week immersive programme combining (a) a three-week placement at a Taiwanese NGO (event preparation, project management, fundraising activities), (b) workshops on project management, fundraising and advocacy plus civil society site visits in Taiwan, (c) a roundtable with Taiwanese NGO professionals, and (d) attendance at Taiwan LGBT+ Pride. Fellows propose a project draft for future collaboration with their host NGO at the end of the programme and join an alumni network across Asia. ELIGIBILITY: NGO professionals (community development, environment, education, women's rights, civic engagement and human rights, emergency relief, disability inclusion, children and families welfare, social innovation and entrepreneurship; outstanding applicants from other fields also considered); at least three years of NGO sector experience; aged 25-40; from Southeast Asia (Brunei, Cambodia, Laos, Timor-Leste, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam), South Asia (Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Maldives, Pakistan), Northeast Asia (Japan, South Korea, Mongolia) or Pacific Islands (Palau, Marshall Islands, Tuvalu); proficient in written and oral English (Mandarin a plus). SUPPORT: round-trip airfare, dormitory-style accommodation, meals and local transportation in Taiwan are all fully covered. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium. Programme dates 4 October - 1 November 2026. Deadline 1 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Flaherty: 2026 Fellowship (71st Flaherty Film Seminar) - Complimentary Seminar registration for both online and in-person tracks. In-person fellows additionally receive lodging and meals for the duration of the Seminar. The Fellowship does NOT cover travel to and from Vermont. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium. - deadline 09 Jun 2026
item.description:
The Flaherty

Location: Vermont, USA (in-person fellows) OR remote (online fellows)

Award: Complimentary Seminar registration for both online and in-person tracks. In-person fellows additionally receive lodging and meals for the duration of the Seminar. The Fellowship does NOT cover travel to and from Vermont. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Programme runs 7-10 October 2026 (in-person fellows arrive 7 October, depart 11 October); pre-Seminar onboarding in early September; written feedback report due 26 October 2026

Deadline: 09 Jun 2026

The Flaherty's 2026 Fellowship programme for the 71st Flaherty Film Seminar, welcoming 15 in-person fellows in Vermont and 15 online fellows. Of these, 8 in-person and 9 online slots are filled through this open call (the remaining slots are underwritten by academic institutions who select recipients internally). Fellows participate in the full Seminar programme, share their work in Fellows' Screenings and Presentations to Seminar programmers and artists, attend meetings and discussions with featured artists and special guests, and may be asked to facilitate breakout discussions. ELIGIBILITY: active in the field through research, filmmaking or related organisational or project-based work; at least 21 years old; has not received an In-Person Flaherty Seminar Fellowship in the last five years; available for the full duration of the programme. No academic background required. The Flaherty actively encourages applications from BIPOC participants. SUPPORT: complimentary Seminar registration (both tracks); in-person fellows additionally receive lodging and meals for the duration of the Seminar. The Fellowship does NOT cover travel to and from Vermont; selected fellows arrange and pay for their own travel. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium. OBLIGATIONS: pre-Seminar onboarding (virtual) in early September; full attendance through the Seminar 7-10 October 2026; written feedback report due 26 October 2026. Apply by 9 June 2026, 23:59 ET.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN America: PEN/Heim Translation Fund Grants 2026 - USD 4,000 per project (up to 10 awards per year). No application fee. - deadline 15 Jun 2026
item.description:
PEN America

Location: Worldwide (translators of any nationality; project must translate a book-length work into English)

Award: USD 4,000 per project (up to 10 awards per year). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (book-length literary translation in progress)

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

PEN America's annual grants for in-progress book-length literary translations from any language into English. Up to 10 grants of USD 4,000 each. Preference for early-career translators and works from underrepresented languages and regions. Eligible genres include fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry and drama. ELIGIBILITY: translators of any nationality; the project must be a translation of a book-length work into English; previous PEN/Heim recipients are eligible after a waiting period. No application fee. Apply by 15 June 2026 via PEN America's grants portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fondation Jan Michalski: Writers' Residency 2027 - Free residency 2 weeks to 3 months; all travel costs covered; CHF 400/week stipend (approximately USD 475). No application fee. - deadline 31 Aug 2026
item.description:
Fondation Jan Michalski pour l'ecriture et la litterature

Location: Montricher, Switzerland (treehouse-style writers' studios in the Jura foothills)

Award: Free residency 2 weeks to 3 months; all travel costs covered; CHF 400/week stipend (approximately USD 475). No application fee.

Duration: 2 weeks to 3 months in 2027

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Writers' residency at the Fondation Jan Michalski in Montricher, Switzerland (Jura foothills), one of the most generously funded literary residencies in Europe. Open to writers of all nationalities, languages, genres and career stages: poetry, fiction, creative nonfiction, translation, hybrid forms. The residency provides a free stay of 2 weeks to 3 months in the foundation's distinctive treehouse-style writers' studios, plus all travel costs and a CHF 400/week stipend (approximately USD 475). ELIGIBILITY: international writers across all genres and career stages. Applications open 2 June 2026 and close 31 August 2026 for residencies in 2027. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Camargo Foundation: Fellowship 2027-2028 - EUR 3,500 stipend (EUR 350/week x 10 weeks); basic transportation costs; housing in the foundation's apartments for the residency duration. No application fee. - deadline 01 Oct 2026
item.description:
Fondation Camargo

Location: Cassis, France (Mediterranean coast)

Award: EUR 3,500 stipend (EUR 350/week x 10 weeks); basic transportation costs; housing in the foundation's apartments for the residency duration. No application fee.

Duration: 10 weeks in 2027 or 2028

Deadline: 01 Oct 2026

Fondation Camargo's 10-week residency for writers, scholars and artists on the Mediterranean coast in Cassis, France. About 14 fellows per cycle. Open to writers across genres (poetry, fiction, nonfiction, translation), as well as scholars and artists pursuing serious research, writing or creative projects in the humanities, social sciences and arts. ELIGIBILITY: international writers, scholars and artists; no nationality restriction. SUPPORT: EUR 3,500 stipend (EUR 350/week x 10 weeks); basic transportation costs; housing in the foundation's apartments for the residency duration. The call opens July 2026 and closes 1 October 2026 for residencies in the 2027-2028 cycle. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Translation House Looren: Translators' Residencies and Pro Helvetia Stipends 2027 - Free residency stay (apartments at the translation house). Six competitive CHF 4,000 translation grants per year, each tied to a one-month residency. Pro Helvetia residency programme separately funded. James Joyce Scholarship and Looren Residency 2027 has its own conditions. No application fee. - deadline 15 Oct 2026
item.description:
Uebersetzerhaus Looren

Location: Wernetshausen, Switzerland (Zurich region)

Award: Free residency stay (apartments at the translation house). Six competitive CHF 4,000 translation grants per year, each tied to a one-month residency. Pro Helvetia residency programme separately funded. James Joyce Scholarship and Looren Residency 2027 has its own conditions. No application fee.

Duration: Stays of typically 2 weeks to 2 months; CHF 4,000 stipend grants tied to one-month residencies

Deadline: 15 Oct 2026

Translators' residency at Uebersetzerhaus Looren in Wernetshausen near Zurich. Looren offers a free residency for literary translators with a current contract, including six competitive CHF 4,000 stipend grants per year tied to one-month residencies. The Pro Helvetia residency programme (separately funded) has a 2026-10-15 deadline for 2027 stays. The James Joyce Scholarship and Looren Residency 2027 closes 2026-10-31. General free residencies are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year. ELIGIBILITY: literary translators with substantial published work and a current translation contract; any language combination is welcome. Apply via the application page on looren.net. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Nederlands Letterenfonds: Ontwikkelbeurzen voor Literaire Makers (Development Grants) - EUR 500 to EUR 2,500 per grant. No application fee. - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Nederlands Letterenfonds (Dutch Foundation for Literature)

Location: Netherlands (Dutch-language literary creators - writers, illustrators, translators)

Award: EUR 500 to EUR 2,500 per grant. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Development grants from the Nederlands Letterenfonds (Dutch Foundation for Literature) for starting and advanced Dutch literary creators - writers, illustrators and translators - to fund training, coaching, editorial support, or travel and research abroad linked to a literary project. EUR 500 to EUR 2,500 per grant. ELIGIBILITY: Dutch-language literary creators (Dutch or Flemish-speaking world); see funder's application page for full eligibility criteria. CYCLE: applications are reviewed on a ROLLING basis (the scheme has been open since February 2026 and there is no fixed deadline). No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Nederlands Letterenfonds: Project Grants for Literary Translators (August 2026 round) - Project-based subsidy. Income cap EUR 52,500 for primary income. Standard minimum rates: EUR 0.0802/word for prose; EUR 2.92/line for poetry. No application fee. - deadline 15 Aug 2026
item.description:
Nederlands Letterenfonds (Dutch Foundation for Literature)

Location: Worldwide (literary translators with a contracted translation from or into Dutch)

Award: Project-based subsidy. Income cap EUR 52,500 for primary income. Standard minimum rates: EUR 0.0802/word for prose; EUR 2.92/line for poetry. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based, scaled to the contracted translation

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

Project subsidies from the Nederlands Letterenfonds for advanced literary translators with a current publishing contract for a translation from or into Dutch. The next round closes 15 August 2026; decisions are made approximately four months after the deadline (December). ELIGIBILITY: literary translators with a substantial published portfolio and a contracted translation project; primary income must be below EUR 52,500. SUPPORT: project-based subsidy calculated against per-word and per-line minimum rates (EUR 0.0802/word for prose, EUR 2.92/line for poetry). Apply via the funder's translator portal on the linked page. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Vertalershuis Amsterdam (Translators' House): Residency - Private apartment for 2 weeks to 2 months, plus EUR 1,000/month grant (approximately EUR 115/month deducted to cover utilities). No application fee. - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Nederlands Letterenfonds (Vertalershuis Amsterdam)

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands (five private apartments at the Vertalershuis)

Award: Private apartment for 2 weeks to 2 months, plus EUR 1,000/month grant (approximately EUR 115/month deducted to cover utilities). No application fee.

Duration: 2 weeks to 2 months

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Residency for literary translators of Dutch literature into any language, hosted by the Nederlands Letterenfonds at the Vertalershuis (Translators' House) in Amsterdam. Five private apartments, available for stays of 2 weeks to 2 months. Residents receive a EUR 1,000/month grant (approximately EUR 115/month deducted to cover utilities) on top of the accommodation. CYCLE: applications accepted on a ROLLING basis. ELIGIBILITY: literary translators on the Letterenfonds or Flanders Literature approved-translators list with a current publishing contract for a Dutch-to-foreign-language translation. Apply via the page linked below. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Lab: Living Wage Residencies 2026-27 (San Francisco) - USD 17,530 artist fee per resident (commensurate with three months of work at a San Francisco Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator) plus USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials. Three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space, plus two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces for the residency outcome. Curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. Stage-2 finalists receive an additional USD 250 stipend to develop their full proposal. No application fee. - deadline 31 May 2026
item.description:
The Lab

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, USA (eligibility restricted to artists based in the Bay Area; significant in-person access to The Lab's space in the Mission District is required)

Award: USD 17,530 artist fee per resident (commensurate with three months of work at a San Francisco Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator) plus USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials. Three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space, plus two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces for the residency outcome. Curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. Stage-2 finalists receive an additional USD 250 stipend to develop their full proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies take place between July 2026 and June 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

The Lab's flagship Living Wage Residency, a substantial in-person residency in San Francisco's Mission District for artists working in any media and discipline. Since 2014 the programme has directed over USD 1 million to artists, with artist fees representing nearly 30% of The Lab's average operating budget. The Lab's history is as a site for experimental and interdisciplinary work spanning performance, exhibition, installation, sound and experimental music, writing, institutional critique, social practice, parades, feasts and further disciplines yet to be defined. The Lab's mission centres traditionally underrepresented artists, particularly those identifying as BIPOC, LGBTQ+, women, disabled and/or from economically marginalised backgrounds. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in the San Francisco Bay Area (tight geographic restriction; in-person access to The Lab's space is central to the residency). SUPPORT per residency: USD 17,530 artist fee (3 months at a Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator); USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials; three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space (as available around other programming); two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces; curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. PROCESS: Stage 1 application (CV, 3 work links, brief project description and resource plan) due 31 May 2026; Stage 2 invites up to 12 selected artists to develop a full proposal with a USD 250 stipend (Stage 2 deadline TBD). Reviewed by a diverse panel of professional artists and curators plus The Lab's Board. Outside funding additionally secured for a proposal is not factored into the decision, but The Lab may help identify further sources. Supported by the San Francisco Arts Commission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Video Pool: Video Commission Residency 2026 - VCR Vol.4 (Attrition) - $1,500 artist fee per selected artist (currency not explicitly stated; likely CAD as Video Pool is Winnipeg-based). Artists based in Winnipeg additionally receive access to VP's facilities and equipment (valued at $1,500). Final works added to Video Pool's distribution catalogue and archive. - deadline 14 Jun 2026
item.description:
Video Pool Media Arts Centre

Location: Remote (no in-person attendance required); final showcase at Video Pool's Poolside Gallery in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada. Equipment/facilities access only for artists based in Winnipeg.

Award: $1,500 artist fee per selected artist (currency not explicitly stated; likely CAD as Video Pool is Winnipeg-based). Artists based in Winnipeg additionally receive access to VP's facilities and equipment (valued at $1,500). Final works added to Video Pool's distribution catalogue and archive.

Duration: Residency period 6 July - 11 September 2026; exhibition September 2026

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Video Pool Media Arts Centre's 4th Video Commission Residency, VCR Vol.4 'Attrition', commissioning new experimental single-channel video works (5-20 minutes) responding to the theme of attrition: conditions shaped by sustained contact and duration, in images, materials and environments, where form and meaning change through accumulating processes. Artists are encouraged to consider how images and environments are worn down through use, working with analog or digital processes (or both) to explore feedback systems, compression, copying, translation, repeated handling and similar processes that produce difference with each iteration. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists (no nationality restriction stated). No in-person attendance required during the creation process - effectively remote-friendly. SUPPORT: $1,500 artist fee per selected artist; artists based in Winnipeg additionally receive access to VP's facilities and equipment (valued at $1,500). Final works showcased at VP's Poolside Gallery in September 2026 and added to VP's distribution catalogue and archive, with the distribution department promoting work for exhibitions, screenings, open calls and long-term preservation. Application materials: brief project description, CV and recent or related work via the form on the linked page. Deadline 14 June 2026, 23:59. Residency period 6 July - 11 September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Conference Fund - From USD 10,000 per grant (no preset upper cap stated). No application fee. - deadline 31 Aug 2026
item.description:
Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide (subject to legal eligibility by country)

Award: From USD 10,000 per grant (no preset upper cap stated). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based. Processing approximately 3 months from submission to decision, plus 2-3 weeks for finalisation before disbursement.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Wikimedia Foundation grants supporting the organisation of local, regional and thematic conferences that bring Wikimedians together for knowledge sharing, skill development and professional networking. Two rounds per year. Round 1 deadline: 31 August 2026. Round 2 deadline: 1 February 2027. ELIGIBILITY: groups, organisations and Wikimedia affiliates; individuals may apply via a fiscal sponsor. SUPPORT: from USD 10,000 per grant; processing time approximately 3 months from submission to decision, plus 2-3 weeks for finalisation before fund disbursement. No application fee. Apply via the page at meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Grants:Conference.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Community Fund: General Support Fund (2026-27 cycles) - Minimum USD 10,000 per year; regional maximums vary (e.g., up to USD 500,000/year in North America). The 2024-25 average for first-time grantees was approximately USD 33,000 globally. No application fee. - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide; reviewed through regional committees (e.g., North America, CEE, MENA, Sub-Saharan Africa, etc.)

Award: Minimum USD 10,000 per year; regional maximums vary (e.g., up to USD 500,000/year in North America). The 2024-25 average for first-time grantees was approximately USD 33,000 globally. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 6 months of programming; typically 12 months, with multi-year options available

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Wikimedia Foundation's largest community-side grant: multi-month or annual programming budgets supporting education, cultural heritage, gender and diversity, community engagement, free-knowledge advocacy and other Wikimedia-aligned work. ELIGIBILITY: individuals, groups, Wikimedia affiliates and organisations from the Wikimedia movement; grantees who currently hold a General Support Fund cannot simultaneously hold a Rapid Fund. AMOUNTS: minimum USD 10,000/year; regional maximums vary (e.g., up to USD 500,000/year for North America); the 2024-25 average for first-time grantees was about USD 33,000 globally. DURATION: minimum 6 months of programming; typically 12 months, with multi-year options. CYCLES: two rounds per year, processed by regional review committees. Round 2 (2025-26) deadlines passed in early March 2026. Round 1 (2026-27) deadlines typically fall in late August / early September 2026, with exact dates varying by region - confirm the regional deadline on the linked page before applying. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Hub Fund - No preset minimum or maximum. Historical reference: research initiatives around USD 25,000; pilot-year hubs typically USD 50,000-110,000. Total pool USD 700,000 for the July 2025 - June 2026 fiscal cycle. No application fee. - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide (groups working on Wikimedia hub initiatives)

Award: No preset minimum or maximum. Historical reference: research initiatives around USD 25,000; pilot-year hubs typically USD 50,000-110,000. Total pool USD 700,000 for the July 2025 - June 2026 fiscal cycle. No application fee.

Duration: Varies by stage: pre-pilot, pilot (approximately 1 year), or operations phases

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Wikimedia Foundation grants supporting hub initiatives - regional or thematic structures that coordinate Wikimedia work across multiple affiliates or contributors. Funds may cover staffing, programming, community support and operational expenses for hubs at pre-pilot, pilot or operational stages. ELIGIBILITY: groups only (not individuals). Applicants must be Wikimedia Movement groups composed of Wikimedia affiliates, unaffiliated organisations, organisers and/or project contributors working on a hub initiative. AMOUNTS: no preset cap; research initiatives historically around USD 25,000, pilot-year hubs typically USD 50,000-110,000; total pool USD 700,000 for the July 2025 - June 2026 fiscal cycle. CYCLES: four quarterly rounds per annual pool (Round 1: 1 August; Round 2: 1 November; Round 3: 1 February; Round 4: 1 April). The 2025-26 cycle's final round closed 1 April 2026; the next annual pool (2026-27) is expected to follow the same quarterly pattern, with Round 1 likely 1 August 2026 - confirm on the linked page. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Research Fund (2027 cycle expected) - Requests must exceed USD 2,000. Type 1 and Type 3 proposals: maximum USD 50,000. Type 2 proposals (extended research initiatives): maximum USD 150,000, capped at USD 75,000 annually. No application fee. - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide

Award: Requests must exceed USD 2,000. Type 1 and Type 3 proposals: maximum USD 50,000. Type 2 proposals (extended research initiatives): maximum USD 150,000, capped at USD 75,000 annually. No application fee.

Duration: Up to 12 months for Type 1 and Type 3; up to 24 months for Type 2

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Wikimedia Foundation research grants supporting research projects, extended research initiatives and community-building events related to Wikimedia projects, with outputs such as scholarly publications, datasets, or strengthened researcher networks. ELIGIBILITY: individuals, groups and organisations. AMOUNTS: requests must exceed USD 2,000; Type 1 and Type 3 proposals up to USD 50,000; Type 2 (extended research initiatives) up to USD 150,000 with an annual cap of USD 75,000. DURATION: up to 12 months for Type 1 and Type 3; up to 24 months for Type 2. CYCLE STATUS: the 2026 cycle is fully closed (Letter of Intent 16 January 2026, full proposal 3 April 2026, final notification 1 May 2026). The next annual cycle is expected to follow the same pattern, with a Letter of Intent deadline around January 2027 - check the linked page for the confirmed 2027 timeline. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IT for Change: FemFirst Research Observatory 2026 (Feminist AI Futures in South and Southeast Asia) - Approximately CAD 12,000 per proposal for a 12-month period - deadline 15 Jun 2026
item.description:
IT for Change

Location: South and Southeast Asia preferred; applicants located in other countries are considered if the research focuses on the region

Award: Approximately CAD 12,000 per proposal for a 12-month period

Duration: 12 months

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

The FemFirst Research Observatory supports mid- to senior-career women and non-binary-identifying researchers and scholars in contributing original research on feminist AI futures in South and Southeast Asia. The Observatory will support 8 to 12 researchers, each developing two high-quality original research papers (6,000 words each) over a 12-month period, alongside two blog syntheses and additional dissemination materials such as podcast episodes. AIMS: build a robust body of empirical, conceptual, policy and applied research at the intersection of AI and gender equality in South and Southeast Asia; open new technical, social and political pathways for feminist AI; and seed an interdisciplinary AI knowledge ecosystem and network of researchers and institutions in the region. ELIGIBILITY: established mid- to senior-career researchers and scholars; may be independent researchers, individual researchers, scholars or academics, or research teams at registered research institutions or entities (universities, research centres, non-profit organisations, for-profit firms). Applicants must demonstrate regional expertise and specialisation in South and Southeast Asia with a track record of work on digitalisation and gender. Applicants must identify as a woman or non-binary person; team applications must be led by a woman or non-binary person. Researchers located outside South or Southeast Asia are considered, but research must focus on the region. Proposed research must be original and not published elsewhere; any additional funding sources for the same proposal must be disclosed. SUPPORT: approximately CAD 12,000 per proposal for a 12-month period; access to a collaborative research network with a shared commitment to feminist principles; mentorship and engagement with leading researchers and practitioners; opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration with academics, builders and developers in the region. Apply via the application form at lnk.itforchange.net/ffroform by 15 June 2026; budget template at lnk.itforchange.net/ffrobudget; contact femfirstlab@itforchange.net.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEA: Grants for Arts Projects (July 2026 cycle) - Project grants typically USD 10,000 to USD 100,000+, with most awards in the USD 10,000-50,000 range; minimum 1:1 cost-share required. - deadline 09 Jul 2026
item.description:
National Endowment for the Arts (NEA)

Location: United States (eligibility for US non-profits with 501(c)(3) status, units of state or local government, or federally-recognised tribal communities)

Award: Project grants typically USD 10,000 to USD 100,000+, with most awards in the USD 10,000-50,000 range; minimum 1:1 cost-share required.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 09 Jul 2026

NEA's flagship project grant for arts organisations across all artistic disciplines. The Grants for Arts Projects (GAP) programme supports projects in dance, design, folk and traditional arts, literary arts, local arts agencies, media arts, museums, music, musical theater, opera, presenting and multidisciplinary works, theater, and visual arts. July 2026 cycle: 'Submit to Grants.gov' deadline 9 July 2026; portal application window 14-21 July 2026. ELIGIBILITY: US non-profits with 501(c)(3) status, units of state or local government, or federally-recognised tribal communities; organisations must have a three-year history of programming and meet a minimum operating budget. Project grants typically USD 10,000 to USD 100,000+, with most awards in the USD 10,000-50,000 range; a 1:1 cost-share is required. item.title: Mozilla Festival (MozFest) 2026: Call for Proposals - Wilding - Limited travel support, not guaranteed: flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform; a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla; a 5-day Metro pass including airport transfer. No additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance. Visa fee reimbursements handled through a separate process. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium. - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Mozilla Foundation

Location: Barcelona, Spain (Recinte Fabra i Coats; co-hosted with the City of Barcelona as part of Open Tech Week)

Award: Limited travel support, not guaranteed: flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform; a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla; a 5-day Metro pass including airport transfer. No additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance. Visa fee reimbursements handled through a separate process. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Three days, 28-30 October 2026

Deadline: Rolling / undated

MozFest 2026 is Mozilla Foundation's annual festival of debates, labs and workshops, forums, showcases, talks and installations - this year at Recinte Fabra i Coats in Barcelona (28-30 October 2026), co-hosted with the City of Barcelona during Open Tech Week. 2026 THEME 'WILDING': a provocation about loosening rigid systems and creating conditions for communities and technologies to evolve collectively, naturally and intuitively. Inspired by ecological rewilding, it explores community-owned mesh networks, federated platforms, open protocols, community archives, and tools built on transparency, trust and mutual flourishing. Mozilla is inviting proposals that exemplify Wilding and celebrate community-led alternatives - work that is proof of what is possible. FESTIVAL TRACKS: Wilding Advocacy; Wilding Knowledge; Wilding Systems; Wilding With Nature; Youth Wilding; Wilding Creativity; Founders Wilding; Developers Wilding. TRAVEL SUPPORT (limited; the festival cannot guarantee support for every applicant): flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform, a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla, and a 5-day Metro pass including the airport. Mozilla cannot provide additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance; visa fee reimbursements are handled through a separate process. There is no artist fee, stipend or honorarium. PROCESS: submit a proposal via the MozFest CFP on the Mozilla Foundation festival site; travel support requests are reviewed after the CFP closes. The CFP closing date is not stated in the linked page - verify the deadline on the MozFest site before applying.

item.title: Heinrich Boell Foundation: Global Majority AI Fellowship 2026 (UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance) - Travel and logistics reimbursement only: international flights, airport transfers, and up to three nights of accommodation in Geneva. Conference registration, accreditation and access facilitated; promotion on the Foundation's social-media channels before the Dialogue. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium. - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Heinrich Boell Foundation Washington, DC

Location: Geneva, Switzerland (first session); applicants must be civil society experts based in countries receiving Official Development Assistance (ODA)

Award: Travel and logistics reimbursement only: international flights, airport transfers, and up to three nights of accommodation in Geneva. Conference registration, accreditation and access facilitated; promotion on the Foundation's social-media channels before the Dialogue. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: First session 6-7 July 2026 in Geneva (2 days), with continued engagement through the second session in New York in May 2027

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Fellowship from the Heinrich Boell Foundation Washington, DC supporting Global Majority civil society experts to participate in the first session of the UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance in Geneva (6-7 July 2026), with continued engagement through the second session in New York in May 2027. The Global Dialogue, established by the UN General Assembly in August 2025 under the Global Digital Compact, is a new multilateral forum to ensure AI governance reflects the priorities of all nations and that the benefits of AI are shared broadly. THEMATIC CLUSTERS at the Global Dialogue: AI opportunities and implications (social, economic, ethical, cultural, linguistic, technical dimensions); bridging AI divides (capacity-building, access, digital foundations); safe, secure and trustworthy AI (interoperability and compatibility of approaches); respecting, protecting and promoting human rights (transparency, accountability, human oversight). The fellowship aims to amplify Global South voices on issues of core relevance to the global green movement - human rights, bias, ecology, labor, conflict - and to improve gender balance in global AI governance. ELIGIBILITY: emerging or experienced leaders working on AI governance, digital rights, data justice, environmental and climate justice or related fields with a focus on the global dimensions of AI policy - activists, local officials, students, researchers and technologists - based in a country receiving Official Development Assistance. WHAT IS OFFERED: international flights, airport transfers, and up to three nights of accommodation in Geneva are reimbursed; conference registration, accreditation and access are facilitated; selected fellows are promoted on the Foundation's social-media channels before the Dialogue. There is no artist fee, stipend or honorarium. WHAT IS EXPECTED: travel to Geneva and active participation on 6-7 July 2026; fellows are encouraged to participate in activities ahead of the 2027 New York session; provide a short statement on the issues they hope to see addressed at the Dialogue and reflect on the experience; engage actively with the Foundation over social media before, during and after the Dialogue; be part of an emerging network of civil society experts working to strengthen AI governance globally. PROCESS: applicants must (1) apply to the official UN AI Dialogue 2026 Call for Participation Support, AND (2) send application materials to the Foundation via Drew.Mitnick[at]us.boell.org and Mariama.Balde[at]us.boell.org. Materials: standard resume/CV (max 2 pages, including country of residence and gender; no photo or marital status) plus a motivational statement (max 300 words) on the biggest challenge for AI governance right now, how to address it, current steps being taken, and how Dialogue participation will advance those goals. Applications accepted on a rolling basis.

Open call details

item.title: FILE NOT FOUND: Open Call for Archive Residencies - EUR 4,000 (EUR 2,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 production budget). Travel and accommodation covered up to EUR 2,000 for one person. - deadline 07 Jun 2026
item.description:
Goethe-Institut

Location: On-site at ten cultural archives around the world; final exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany

Award: EUR 4,000 (EUR 2,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 production budget). Travel and accommodation covered up to EUR 2,000 for one person.

Duration: On-site residency of 2 weeks to 2 months, scheduled between August 2026 and May 2027

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Open call to participate in FILE NOT FOUND, a project exploring the risks and challenges archives face as sites of cultural memory, resistant knowledge and alternative perspectives under growing political pressure. Ten practitioners - artists, thinkers, activists, theatre makers, dancers, musicians and more - will be selected for residencies at ten cultural archives around the world. On site, residents engage with existing archival practices, explore both safe and not-so-safe spaces, investigate traditional and experimental methods of archiving, and ideally develop artistically subversive approaches that strengthen the resilience of archives against external influence. ELIGIBILITY: artists worldwide, all disciplines. Applicants review the ten participating archives and select the one they wish to apply to. The on-site residency runs 2 weeks to 2 months between August 2026 and May 2027. SUPPORT: EUR 4,000 (EUR 2,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 production budget), with travel and accommodation covered up to EUR 2,000 for one person. OUTCOME: an artwork plus a residency report, with a group exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany in autumn 2027. Working language English. Application deadline 7 June 2026, 12pm GMT+1.

item.title: Passage Art-Residency-Programme 2026 (KOBRA Cultural Network) - Per residency place: accommodation for the four-week stay; a grant of EUR 5,000; artists in residence with a child receive an additional EUR 1,000 toward temporary childcare. Travel, local transport, consumables and living expenses are paid by the artist; payment is made on receipt of an invoice. - deadline 30 May 2026
item.description:
Polygona Kunstverein e.V. (KOBRA Cultural Network)

Location: Wittenberg region, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany

Award: Per residency place: accommodation for the four-week stay; a grant of EUR 5,000; artists in residence with a child receive an additional EUR 1,000 toward temporary childcare. Travel, local transport, consumables and living expenses are paid by the artist; payment is made on receipt of an invoice.

Duration: Four weeks, October 2026

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Two residency places in the Passage Art-Residency-Programme run by Polygona Kunstverein e.V. as part of the KOBRA Cultural Network, which explores continuities and discontinuities in the cultural landscape between Flaeming and Duebener Heide in the district of Wittenberg (Saxony-Anhalt, Germany). National and international artists working in photography and film are invited to apply. The two 2026 residencies run in October 2026 in collaboration with KOBRA partners Ferropolis Foundation for Industrial Heritage and the Research Library of Reformation Studies Wittenberg (RFB). TOPIC ONE (Ferropolis): artistic perspectives on the history of the former Zschornewitz coal-fired power station, the transformation of industrial landscapes, and visions for the future of post-industrial areas. TOPIC TWO (RFB): exploring the research library as a store of knowledge and examining the production of knowledge and its visual representation, drawing on a collection of around 230,000 volumes including Reformation-era first editions, manuscripts and historical writings. ELIGIBILITY: minimum age 18, no age limit; students are not eligible. Artists are selected by jury on the quality of submitted work; selected artists notified from 20 June 2026. FUNDING per place: accommodation for the four-week stay, a EUR 5,000 grant, and an additional EUR 1,000 for childcare if attending with a child. OBLIGATIONS: four weeks present in October 2026, realisation of an artistic project, provision of ten project photographs by 31 December 2026, delivery of a one-day workshop for young people, a public artist talk, and two days participating in filming for a documentary. Artists retain copyright and ownership of works created. Childcare and child-friendly accommodation can be arranged; partners may also accompany the artist at the artist's own additional cost. No application fee. Apply via the online form at passageair.org by 30 May 2026; applications accepted in German or English.

Open call details

item.title: Creapolis: Malta 2026 - Diversification through Culture - Fully funded: all travel, accommodation and programme costs are covered. - deadline 04 Jun 2026
item.description:
Creapolis (B Creative Tracks)

Location: Malta (open to Ukrainian professionals currently based in Ukraine)

Award: Fully funded: all travel, accommodation and programme costs are covered.

Duration: One week, 5-9 October 2026

Deadline: 04 Jun 2026

One-week immersive programme in Malta on 'diversification through culture', examining how cultural value is produced, negotiated and redistributed across institutions, public space, tourism, education and community networks. The edition focuses on processes, decision-making structures and systemic dynamics in a dense, interconnected cultural ecosystem, where culture operates simultaneously as heritage, contemporary practice, community expression, public service and economic activity. Programme themes: diversification of cultural practice across heritage, craft, contemporary arts and community traditions; hybrid governance models; culture as a driver of tourism and place-based economies; cultural transmission and identity-building; collaboration between public authorities, civil society and practitioners; adaptive cultural strategies in resource-constrained environments. Participants get hands-on experience, practical tools and collaborative methodologies, exchange with local experts and European peers, exchange with other Ukrainian professionals, and access to a European network for continued cooperation. ELIGIBILITY: Ukrainian professionals currently based in Ukraine, engaged in community and civic development through culture, education, innovation or entrepreneurship - including civil servants and local policymakers, educators, researchers and social innovators, cultural and creative entrepreneurs, and community organisers and local development actors. Applicants should demonstrate clear motivation, relevant experience and a commitment to applying insights to their local Ukrainian context. Working language English. All travel, accommodation and programme costs covered. Deadline 4 June 2026, 5pm CEST.

Open call details

item.title: MedSEA: Socially Engaged Arts in the Mediterranean - Open Call 2026 - Full support: travel, accommodation, artistic fee, mentorship and access to an international network of artists and cultural professionals. - deadline 05 Jun 2026
item.description:
Larnaka 2030, Al Badil, Liv.in.g and Busart (Creative Europe consortium)

Location: Residencies in Larnaka (Cyprus) and Sfax (Tunisia); applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe-eligible country

Award: Full support: travel, accommodation, artistic fee, mentorship and access to an international network of artists and cultural professionals.

Duration: Multi-phase programme October 2026 - May 2027: capacity-building phase plus a four-week residency (Larnaka 5 October - 1 November 2026; Sfax 5 April - 2 May 2027)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

MedSEA (Socially Engaged Arts in the Mediterranean Area) is a Creative Europe cooperation project where artistic practices engage directly with communities, territories and the social transformations shaping the Mediterranean. This open call selects 12 emerging artists and cultural professionals working - or wishing to work - with participatory and socially engaged artistic practices. It is a process of co-creation in real contexts rather than a traditional residency: participants work directly with local communities developing territory-rooted artistic projects. The programme runs in phases - a preparation and capacity-building phase (webinars and workshops on community-based artistic practices, sustainability and cultural project design), then a four-week residency of fieldwork and co-creation supported by project partners. Residencies take place in Larnaka, Cyprus (5 October - 1 November 2026) and Sfax, Tunisia (5 April - 2 May 2027); resulting works are presented at the Mahallart Festival in Larnaka and the Hors-Lits Festival in Sfax. ELIGIBILITY: artists and cultural professionals aged 18 or above, legally residing in a country eligible under the Creative Europe Programme, and actively engaged in community-based or socially engaged artistic practices across disciplines such as visual arts, performing arts, interdisciplinary practices and public space interventions. Selected participants receive full support: travel, accommodation, artistic fee, mentorship and network access. Implemented by a consortium of Larnaka 2030 (Cyprus), Al Badil (Tunisia), Liv.in.g (Italy) and Busart (Greece); co-funded by the Creative Europe Programme of the European Union. Deadline 5 June 2026, 23:59 CET. Contact: maria@larnaka2030.eu.

Open call details

item.description:
ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen) - Alexander Rave Foundation

Location: Host institutions in Germany (applicants must be cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list)

Award: Net payment of EUR 1,500 per month; monthly family allowances for accompanying family (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance (including family); monthly public transport pass for the city of residence; up to EUR 500 for a language course.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Scholarship from ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen), supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, on the management of cultural heritage and the transformation of museums and exhibition centres. It enables cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list to stay with host institutions in order to deepen their professional knowledge transnationally, introduce new perspectives and build long-term cooperation through exchange. The focus is on transcultural dialogue and engaging with cultural heritage, collections and archive records, funding mutual knowledge transfer and the creation of sustainable networks, and supporting freedom in art and science, freedom of opinion and open debates. Thematic focal areas centre on transforming museums and exhibition venues in line with the ICOM definition of museums (2023): cultural participation, sustainability, non-discriminatory spaces and decolonial working methods. ELIGIBILITY: curators, restorers, mediators and culture managers from DAC-list countries. BENEFITS: net payment of EUR 1,500/month; monthly family allowances (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance including family; monthly public transport pass; up to EUR 500 for a language course. Application deadline 30 June 2026 for scholarships beginning after 1 February 2027.

Open call details

item.title: Gasworks Residency Open Call 2026 (Artists based in Southern Africa) - Fully funded residency: single-room accommodation in Gasworks' Residency House (shared two-storey house with three communal bathrooms and a kitchen); economy return flights and taxi transfers from the artist's home country to London (one cabin + one hold bag); all visa costs and visa application support; weekly stipend of £175 for living expenses; materials budget up to £800; prepaid TFL zones 1+2 travel card for unlimited central London travel throughout the residency. - deadline 18 May 2026
item.description:
Gasworks

Location: London, United Kingdom (residency held at Gasworks; applicants must be based in Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia, South Africa or Zimbabwe)

Award: Fully funded residency: single-room accommodation in Gasworks' Residency House (shared two-storey house with three communal bathrooms and a kitchen); economy return flights and taxi transfers from the artist's home country to London (one cabin + one hold bag); all visa costs and visa application support; weekly stipend of £175 for living expenses; materials budget up to £800; prepaid TFL zones 1+2 travel card for unlimited central London travel throughout the residency.

Duration: Eleven weeks, 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: 18 May 2026

Eleven-week fully funded residency at Gasworks in London for an early-career artist based in Southern Africa (Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia, South Africa, Zimbabwe). Gasworks' residency programme supports professional development, cultural exchange, and artistic experimentation, hosting up to twenty residencies a year. Residencies are self-led, non-prescriptive and process-based: artists are expected to be self-motivated and to lead on the research and production of their own work, while Gasworks provides administrative, pastoral and curatorial support. Includes 24/7 access to a fully accessible private studio at Gasworks alongside resident artists and a community of London-based studio holders. ELIGIBILITY: early-career artist based in Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia, South Africa, or Zimbabwe. SUPPORT: single-room accommodation in the Gasworks Residency House; economy return flights and taxi transfers from the artist's home country to London (one cabin + one hold bag); all visa costs and visa application support; weekly stipend of £175; materials budget up to £800; prepaid TFL zones 1+2 travel card. Deadline 18 May 2026, 1pm UK time.

item.title: Magnetic 5: Franco-UK Artist Residencies (2026 call) - Threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500, plus accommodation, access to a workspace, curatorial mentoring and networking opportunities. - deadline 02 Jun 2026
item.description:
Fluxus Art Projects

Location: France and United Kingdom (artists from a French region apply to the associated UK residency; artists from a UK nation apply to the associated French region)

Award: Threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500, plus accommodation, access to a workspace, curatorial mentoring and networking opportunities.

Duration: Two to three months (residency dates set per host)

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

Magnetic is a joint Franco-UK initiative coordinated by Fluxus Art Projects, bringing together ten institutions for a programme of 2-3 month artist residencies: five in France (Capc, Bordeaux; Frac Grand Large, Dunkirk; Frac Bretagne, Rennes; Villa Arson, Nice; Betonsalon, Paris) and five in the UK (Wysing Arts Centre and Gasworks in England; Flax Art Studios, Northern Ireland; Aberystwyth Arts Centre, Wales; Cove Park, Scotland). Artists from each of the five French regions apply for the associated UK residency; artists from each UK nation apply for the associated French region. Societal and environmental issues are at the heart of the programme; the residencies are situated, with selected artists expected to work in resonance with each host's geographical, historical, societal and artistic context. SUPPORT: threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500; accommodation; access to workspace; curatorial mentoring; networking opportunities. Deadline Tuesday 2 June 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Templeton Foundation: Religion, Science & Society - Intelligence (2026 cycle) - Two tiers: focused studies under USD $260K; larger collaborative initiatives up to USD $2.5M (using subgrants or internal RFP structures). - deadline 15 Jul 2026
item.description:
John Templeton Foundation

Location: Worldwide (Templeton funds charitable organizations inside and outside the US)

Award: Two tiers: focused studies under USD $260K; larger collaborative initiatives up to USD $2.5M (using subgrants or internal RFP structures).

Duration: Three years

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Religion, Science & Society (RSS) funding area, 2026 cycle, Intelligence track. In alignment with the 2026 Foundation-wide Intelligence Venture, RSS welcomes three-year proposals applying theological, philosophical, and social-scientific approaches to the guiding themes outlined in the venture description. Two scales of project are supported: small, focused studies under USD $260K, and larger collaborative initiatives up to USD $2.5M that use subgrants or internal Requests For Proposals to structure the work. RSS prioritises interdisciplinary teams. Proposals may include public-engagement components, but the primary activities must be research. Strong fit for projects bringing humanities, social science, and theology into dialogue with AI/cognitive science framings of intelligence. ELIGIBILITY: The Foundation funds charitable organizations only; per Templeton's Apply for a Grant page, 'individuals or applicants from for-profit companies should not apply' (individuals/for-profits are funded only by rare staff invitation). International organizations are eligible; the Foundation has funded organizations from around the world. US applicants are described publicly only as 'charitable organizations' (formal IRS/501(c)(3) verification is handled inside the Templeton Portal after the Funding Inquiry stage); non-US applicants similarly need to be charitable entities, with equivalency handled through the portal. PROCESS: submit an Online Funding Inquiry (OFI) via the Templeton Portal by 15 July 2026; full proposals are by invitation only after OFI review.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Templeton Foundation: Religion, Science & Society - Meaning Making (2026 cycle) - Only two project sizes accepted in this track: (1) projects between USD $700K and $1.5M (proposals over $1M should use a Requests For Proposals (RFP) structure to support competitively selected subgrants around a shared theme); (2) focused studies under USD $260K investigating a specific dimension of meaning-making. - deadline 15 Jul 2026
item.description:
John Templeton Foundation

Location: Worldwide (Templeton funds charitable organizations inside and outside the US)

Award: Only two project sizes accepted in this track: (1) projects between USD $700K and $1.5M (proposals over $1M should use a Requests For Proposals (RFP) structure to support competitively selected subgrants around a shared theme); (2) focused studies under USD $260K investigating a specific dimension of meaning-making.

Duration: Three years

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Religion, Science & Society (RSS) funding area, 2026 cycle, Meaning Making track. RSS is especially interested in three-year projects that investigate concrete manifestations of meaning making in a world marked by rapid social change, with attention to the practices, experiences, and transitions that enable people to develop, sustain, or renew a life of meaning. STRICT BUDGET WINDOWS: this track will consider ONLY (1) projects between USD $700K and $1.5M (and proposals over $1M must use a Requests For Proposals (RFP) structure with competitively selected subgrants around a shared theme), or (2) focused studies requesting less than USD $260K on a specific dimension of meaning-making. Proposals outside these windows will not be considered. RSS prioritises interdisciplinary teams. Proposals may include public-engagement components, but the primary activities must be research. ELIGIBILITY: The Foundation funds charitable organizations only; per Templeton's Apply for a Grant page, 'individuals or applicants from for-profit companies should not apply' (individuals/for-profits are funded only by rare staff invitation). International organizations are eligible; the Foundation has funded organizations from around the world. US applicants are described publicly only as 'charitable organizations' (formal IRS/501(c)(3) verification is handled inside the Templeton Portal after the Funding Inquiry stage); non-US applicants similarly need to be charitable entities, with equivalency handled through the portal. PROCESS: submit an Online Funding Inquiry (OFI) via the Templeton Portal by 15 July 2026; full proposals are by invitation only after OFI review.

John Templeton Foundation

item.title: Weaving Liberation: Digital Justice Fund (Pilot Round 2026) - register by 24 May 2026 - Grants between €10,000 and €50,000 per group, to be used over up to two years (2026-2028). Total Fund budget for this first round: €500,000. - deadline 24 May 2026
item.description:
Weaving Liberation (resourced by Ford Foundation, Luminate, and Oak Foundation)

Location: Europe (Council of Europe member states; at least 50% of the group must be based in one or multiple Council of Europe countries)

Award: Grants between €10,000 and €50,000 per group, to be used over up to two years (2026-2028). Total Fund budget for this first round: €500,000.

Duration: Up to two years of project work, between 2026 and 2028

Deadline: 24 May 2026

The Digital Justice Fund works towards nurturing an ecosystem where groups resisting violent technological harms and imagining more nourishing relationships with technologies have the resources they need to dream, organise, build and thrive towards liberatory digital presents and futures. We are interested in funding: Community building and sustaining around digital justice issues: community organising, peer-to-peer support, relationship-building activities, etc. Knowledge and capacity building: popular and political education, investigative journalism, skills exchanges, issue mappings (e.g., technological harms, community needs and desires), advancing liberatory narratives around technology, artistic interventions, and other creative approaches, etc. Coalition and movement building: advocacy, strategic litigation, campaigning, etc. Tech-related mutual aid: tech repair circles, peer support on digital safety and security, shared technical infrastructures for movement work, software/hardware sharing initiatives, etc. Life-affirming technology work: prototyping, piloting, or expanding community-centred, transfeminist, anti-colonial technologies, etc. Key dates: platform registration deadline Sunday 24 May 2026, 23:59 CET (registration is required by this date even if application is not yet complete); final application deadline Sunday 21 June 2026, 23:59 CET.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BrightBrain: Artist Residency 2026 (TU Braunschweig Science & Art Lab) - Artist fee €2,400 per month (gross), plus travel costs and accommodation covered, plus production budget up to €3,500 (gross). - deadline 25 May 2026
item.description:
Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium

Location: Braunschweig, Germany (in-person residency at TU Braunschweig)

Award: Artist fee €2,400 per month (gross), plus travel costs and accommodation covered, plus production budget up to €3,500 (gross).

Duration: Autumn/winter 2026 residency (start from September 2026, exact start date flexible). Length agreed with host.

Deadline: 25 May 2026

Open call for an international artist-in-residence at the Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium, as part of the Ecoversity initiative. The residency aims to connect artistic and scientific perspectives. Artists are invited to propose their own research-based projects related to microphotonics, biomedicine, technology, neurodegenerative diseases, neurobiology, neuromorphic computing, and microelectronics (the current resident is collaborating with the Nitride Technology Center on light transmission in digital applications including micro-LEDs and neuromorphic computing). Strong fit for art-science, sensory translation, critical-data, and neuroscience-informed practices. Eligibility: international artists; should show interest in biomedicine, technology and innovation in their artistic work; be comfortable working in interdisciplinary environments; be motivated to collaborate with experts from science, culture and education; be willing to participate in two public events during the residency; and be able to communicate in English. Application by PDF to artist-residency@tu-braunschweig.de with subject 'BrightBrain residency' (if files exceed 25 MB, upload via the TU Braunschweig Nextcloud link in the call). Application deadline 25 May 2026. Jury decision expected end of June 2026; residency starts from September 2026 (exact date flexible). Free to apply.

Open call details

item.title: Banff Centre: Visual Arts Thematic Residency - Future Figurations (2027) - Paid program. Total before scholarships: CAD $9,484.55 per person. With the Banff Centre Arts Scholarship (100% tuition + 25% accommodation/meals): CAD $4,384.73 per person. Canadian Indigenous Scholarship: 100% tuition + accommodation + meals (no out-of-pocket). Application fee: CAD $65 (individuals/groups), CAD $35 for Indigenous applicants. No artist stipend. - deadline 03 Jun 2026
item.description:
Banff Centre for Arts and Creativity

Location: Banff, Alberta, Canada (in-person residency on the Banff Centre campus in the Canadian Rockies)

Award: Paid program. Total before scholarships: CAD $9,484.55 per person. With the Banff Centre Arts Scholarship (100% tuition + 25% accommodation/meals): CAD $4,384.73 per person. Canadian Indigenous Scholarship: 100% tuition + accommodation + meals (no out-of-pocket). Application fee: CAD $65 (individuals/groups), CAD $35 for Indigenous applicants. No artist stipend.

Duration: 5 weeks total (arrival 24 January 2027, program 25 January-26 February 2027, departure 27 February 2027)

Deadline: 03 Jun 2026

Visual Arts Thematic Residency at Banff Centre led by artist-faculty Rajni Perera and Marigold Santos. Drawing on ideas of speculative futures, science fiction, myth-making, and world-building, artists are invited to research, imagine and create possible selves and possible worlds. The program considers futurity as an anti-oppressive and restorative force, exploring new possibilities of selfhood and agency by decolonising ideas of the body. Open to international applicants aged 18+ with post-secondary formal training or equivalent experience, working in visual arts disciplines: ceramics, digital media, painting, drawing, printmaking, papermaking, photography, sculpture, installation. Individual artists or duos (no more than two collaborators). Priority given to artists who have not attended a Banff visual arts residency in the past three years. Residency includes single bedroom on-campus accommodation, meal plan (~$68/day credit equivalent), individual studio space with shared production facility access, faculty mentorship and workshops, and access to Banff Centre facilities (fitness centre, library, performances). IMPORTANT: this is a paid program, not a stipend-paying residency. Total before scholarships is CAD $9,484.55; the Banff Centre Arts Scholarship (covering 100% tuition + 25% accommodation/meals) brings the out-of-pocket cost to CAD $4,384.73. A Canadian Indigenous Scholarship covers tuition, accommodation and meals in full. There is also a non-refundable application fee (CAD $65 standard, $35 Indigenous). Apply via the Banff Centre online portal with materials uploaded through SlideRoom.

Open call details

item.title: WIELS: Residency Programme 2027 (Brussels) - Artistic recognition grant of €3,000 per selected artist (4 artists selected). Awarded as an artistic distinction without any production obligation or service in return. Under Belgian tax law, artistic prizes may be treated as miscellaneous income and may benefit from a partial tax exemption within certain limits (depends on the beneficiary's individual situation). - deadline 31 May 2026
item.description:
WIELS

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at WIELS contemporary art centre)

Award: Artistic recognition grant of €3,000 per selected artist (4 artists selected). Awarded as an artistic distinction without any production obligation or service in return. Under Belgian tax law, artistic prizes may be treated as miscellaneous income and may benefit from a partial tax exemption within certain limits (depends on the beneficiary's individual situation).

Duration: Six months in 2027, choose one block: January-June 2027 OR July-December 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Annual residency programme at WIELS (Brussels-based centre for contemporary art). This call is open EXCLUSIVELY to Belgian artists or artists who have been living and working in Belgium for at least three years, and who have contributed to the Brussels contemporary art scene during that period. An independent jury selects four artists, each awarded a €3,000 artistic recognition grant. The grant is a distinction supporting the quality of the artistic practice; there is no production requirement or contractual service obligation attached. Residency period is six months in 2027 (January-June or July-December block, applicant choice). Application deadline 31 May 2026. Application materials via online form: personal information; artistic approach (max 250 words); statement of intent describing projects, ideas and expectations for the residency (max 250 words); three professional references (name, email, phone, profession); and portfolio + CV via Option 1 (Dropbox/Google Drive link with up to 10 images/videos plus a numbered descriptive list with title, date, technique, dimensions and ~50-word description per item, plus CV) or Option 2 (PDF with up to 10 captioned images plus CV). Videos may also be hosted on YouTube/Vimeo (with password if needed); videos of still works are not accepted. All share links must be set to 'anyone with the link can view'; links requiring access requests will not be considered. Questions: residency@wiels.org (do not email about results, notifications will be sent). Free to apply. Note: this is a Belgium-restricted call and is not open to artists without a Belgian / 3+ years-in-Belgium connection.

Open call details

Two structures: fixed grant of €2,480 per month for 1-6 month work periods, OR a flexible grant of up to 12 months with the amount determined by project plan and budget. - deadline 15 Oct 2026
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands (applicants must be part of the professional visual art field in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom)

Award: Up to 70% of eligible expenses. Round budget: €229,000. Two structures: fixed grant of €2,480 per month for 1-6 month work periods, OR a flexible grant of up to 12 months with the amount determined by project plan and budget.

Duration: 1 to 12 months, depending on project plan

Deadline: 15 Oct 2026

Mondriaan Fund grant for written and/or spoken-word publications about contemporary visual art: article series, long reads, podcasts, video essays, social-content series, and similar formats published across magazines, newspapers, online platforms, public media or social media. Aimed at strengthening reflection on, criticism of, and journalism around visual art practice in the Netherlands and the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. Eligible applicants: existing or new platforms (online magazines, public media, podcast outlets, etc.) AND individuals working as curators, critics, social-content creators or journalists. New initiatives and collaborations may apply. Two cycles per year; the next deadline is 15 October 2026, 16:00 Dutch time / 10:00 Caribbean time (an earlier 2 April 2026 round has already closed). Grant covers up to 70% of eligible expenses, with two pricing structures: fixed €2,480/month for short 1-6 month work periods, or a flexible grant of up to 12 months for larger project plans (amount determined by submitted plan and budget). Round budget €229,000. Good fit for science-communicator / critical-tech / art-and-AI long-form writing, podcast series, or video essay projects. Applications submitted via the Mondriaan Fund online portal after creating an account; documents cannot be uploaded after the deadline. Free to apply.

item.title: Headlands Center for the Arts: Artist in Residence 2027 (AIR) - Fully sponsored residency: studio space, chef-prepared meals, on-site housing, travel costs, and living-expense stipend. No application fee. - deadline 01 Jun 2026
item.description:
Headlands Center for the Arts

Location: Sausalito, California, United States (Marin Headlands, Golden Gate National Recreation Area)

Award: Fully sponsored residency: studio space, chef-prepared meals, on-site housing, travel costs, and living-expense stipend. No application fee.

Duration: 4 to 10 weeks, scheduled between January and December 2027

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Open call for the 2027 cohort of the Headlands Artist in Residence (AIR) program, one of the longer-running fully-sponsored residencies on the US West Coast. Approximately 50 local, national and international artists are selected each year for a residency of 4 to 10 weeks at the Headlands' Marin Headlands campus (former military buildings inside the Golden Gate National Recreation Area, just north of San Francisco). Residents receive a private studio, chef-prepared meals, on-site housing, travel costs, and a living-expense stipend, fully sponsored by the program. Open to artists at all career stages working in any medium: drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, film, video, new media, installation, fiction and nonfiction writing, poetry, dance, music, interdisciplinary practice, social practice, and architecture. Strong fit for new-media / interdisciplinary / research-driven practices. Application opens 1 April 2026 and closes 1 June 2026 via SlideRoom; applicants are notified of decisions by 11 December 2026. Free to apply.

item.title: Ohme x EXPERIENCE: Open Call - Experience Lab (Digital Artist Residency on Consciousness) - Artist fee: €5,300 (approx. 20 working days based on CP 304 salary scales, max 10 years experience). Production budget up to €2,000. Plus professional documentation, access to EXPERIENCE datasets/tools/documentation, preparatory reading list, Ohme team support, and workspace at USquare. - deadline 31 May 2026
item.description:
Ohme (in collaboration with EXPERIENCE / CRCN, Université libre de Bruxelles)

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at Ohme's premises, USquare, Brussels)

Award: Artist fee: €5,300 (approx. 20 working days based on CP 304 salary scales, max 10 years experience). Production budget up to €2,000. Plus professional documentation, access to EXPERIENCE datasets/tools/documentation, preparatory reading list, Ohme team support, and workspace at USquare.

Duration: 20-day engagement spread between September and November 2026, with public restitution in December 2026. Preparatory period July-August 2026 (reading list and onboarding materials).

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Open call from Ohme (Brussels-based research and curatorial platform at the intersection of art and science) for a digital visual artist or collective to develop an artistic project in residence with EXPERIENCE, an ERC-funded research programme led by Prof. Axel Cleeremans at the Centre for Research in Cognition and Neurosciences (CRCN), Université libre de Bruxelles. EXPERIENCE investigates the nature of subjective experience (phenomenology) and builds 3D maps of 'phenomenal space' from large datasets of similarity and preference judgments. The selected artist works directly with these empirical datasets (similarity judgments, subjective preference data describing how people perceive, compare and value conscious experience) as primary creative material, not as illustration. Possible directions include information design that critically engages with visualising consciousness data; generative or algorithmic works using subjective valuation datasets as compositional grammar; data visualisation as artistic practice; creative coding exploring neural/latent space and perception/emotion/subjectivity; and critical or poetic approaches to measuring/mapping inner experience. Eligibility: visual artist or collective connected to the contemporary art field of the Fédération Wallonie-Bruxelles (live, work, or have a professional practice within the French-speaking art community of Belgium); established practice in digital art, new media, computational art, data visualisation or information design used as artistic practice; experience working with data as creative material (e.g. Processing, p5.js, openFrameworks, TouchDesigner, Max/MSP, or equivalent); critical engagement with the tools/systems behind information production and dissemination; interest in mind/perception/cognition/neuroscience; available in person in Brussels September-November 2026 and for the December restitution; English proficiency (working language); French and/or Dutch appreciated. Ohme is NOT looking for artists who already work on consciousness or neuroscience, but practitioners whose existing methods could be mobilised in this scientific context. Sample EXPERIENCE datasets: https://osf.io/suqkp/overview?view_only=fdc73f1485664a2c9c7571e0c62abe6a (paper: 'Exploring the role of micro-valence in the phenomenal space', https://doi.org/10.1093/nc/niag005). Selection by interdisciplinary jury of EXPERIENCE and Ohme representatives; interviews with top three candidates by phone/video/in-person in late June 2026; selected artist announced late June 2026. Application: single PDF in English to opencall@ohme.be (subject: 'Application Experience Lab') by 31 May 2026, 23:59 CET, including up-to-date CV (English), portfolio (English), 1-page motivation text (English) on interest in the residency and consciousness theme plus technical competences, and the completed contact form (downloadable from Ohme's site). Free to apply.

Open call details
